scope: Derive current operational state without erasing historical queue observations.

source_classes.rule: Treat as the last consolidated queue snapshot at its own blob and timestamp, not as proof that no later additive work exists.
Apply only to the named queue item and owner when the delta is newer than the canonical snapshot and its referenced commits exist.
Use to verify that a claimed delta has source-level support; never infer deployment, runtime success, or peer execution from a commit alone.
Preserve the state observed at the cited source blob. Later records may supersede current interpretation but must not delete the earlier observation.

reconciliation_algorithm: Read the canonical snapshot and retain its blob SHA and updated_at value.
Collect owner-matching queue deltas recorded after that snapshot.
Reject a delta if its owner or queue_item does not match the record it claims to update.
Verify referenced implementation, test, and evidence commits when those identifiers are present.
Order valid deltas by recorded_at, then by commit ancestry when timestamps are equal or ambiguous.
Derive a computed current state without replacing the canonical snapshot or prior continuity records.
Label unexecuted tests, unverified deployments, and unobserved peer runs as unresolved regardless of source commits.

recovered_memory.observed: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE blob predates newer continuity follow-on commits and queue-update records.
recovered_memory.inferred: Reading ACTIVE_QUEUE alone can under-report completed follow-on work; reading only deltas can lose the authoritative baseline and historical context.
recovered_memory.proposed: All teams should compute state from canonical snapshot plus validated owner-scoped deltas until a future explicit consolidation commit is made.
recovered_memory.superseded: The assumption that the latest canonical queue file necessarily contains every later team result.
recovered_memory.unresolved: No atomic multi-writer consolidation protocol or repository-wide delta validator has yet been proven in an authenticated checkout.

privacy.included: Public repository paths, blob SHAs, commit SHAs, timestamps, and abstract operational claims.
privacy.excluded: Raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads.

falsification_or_review_route: Falsify the recovered memory by producing a later explicit consolidation commit whose ACTIVE_QUEUE blob includes the cited follow-on delta, or by showing that the cited queue-update or implementation commit does not exist or does not concern M-001.
